chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.1124 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.6191/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.6192/2025 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 05.05.2025 Mr. Mairaj Ahmed Solangi, advocate for applicant ------------------------------------ Applicant/accused Ameer Bux Wassan son of Shabhar Khan and Rano Khan son of Ameer Bux seek protective bail in FIR No.38/2025, registered at P.S. Rani Pur, District Khairpur Mir’s, for offence under Sections 302, 337H(ii), 34, PPC. Learned counsel for applicants submits that applicants are innocent and they have been falsely implicated in this case for mala fide intentions and ulterior motives; that they are law abiding citizens and intend to surrender before learned trial Court concerned but they apprehend arrest at the hands of local police in order to humiliate and disgrace them, hence they need some protection. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named, for a period of five days from today, subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants/accused are directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the investigation/trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of five days from today or whenever applicants surrender before the trial Court concerned, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicants/accused fail to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, their bail bond shall stand automatically forfeited.

This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of five days from today or whenever applicants surrender before the trial Court concerned, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicants/accused fail to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, their bail bond shall stand automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
